News of Sutskever's departure comes a day after OpenAI launched a new AI model and desktop version of ChatGPT, along with an updated user interface, the company's latest effort to expand use of its popular chatbot.

He's confident that OpenAI "will build [artificial general intelligence] that is both safe and beneficial" under the leadership of CEO Sam Altman, President Greg Brockman and CTO Mira Murati, he continued.
After almost a decade, I have made the decision to leave OpenAI.
In November, OpenAI's board of directors suddenly fired Altman and company President Greg Brockman.
Both Altman and Brockman were reinstated just five days after they were fired, and the original board was disbanded and replaced with a new one.
Sutskever shared that he's moving on to a new project that's "very personally meaningful" to him, though he has yet to share details about it."}]}
